content.value: Sarabeth’s Studio offers vocal training for students interested in musical theater and classical repertoire. The vocal technique taught has an emphasis on anatomy and how to utilize specific muscle groups to free the voice from tension. Currently, all lessons are conducted virtually and in person at her home studio in Altadena, CA.

content.value: Sarabeth Belón
Sarabeth Belón
is a Southern California–based mezzo-soprano known for her expressive artistry, rich tone, and versatility across opera, concert, and oratorio repertoire. She performs regularly with the
Los Angeles Opera Chorus
, both as an ensemble member and featured soloist, and is frequently engaged as an
alto soloist
with major choral ensembles. Recent highlights include appearing as
alto soloist
in Mendelssohn’s
Elijah
with the
La Jolla Symphony & Chorus
(June 2025) and as
alto soloist
in Beethoven’s
Symphony No. 9
with the
San Diego State University Symphony Orchestra
(April 2025). She has also appeared in contemporary works and world premieres, bridging traditional and modern repertoire with equal skill and passion.
Sarabeth’s operatic experience spans both classic and modern repertoire, including
Messiah
Gurrelieder

, and a wide range of contemporary vocal works. Her performances are praised for their technical precision, emotional depth, and musical sensitivity, engaging audiences across Southern California and beyond.
A dedicated educator, Sarabeth serves as an adjunct faculty member in the
Musical Theatre Department at USC’s School of Dramatic Arts

, where she teaches voice using a science-informed, technique-driven approach that helps students build sustainable, versatile voices. She also maintains a thriving private studio, offering virtual and in-person lessons for singers at all levels, empowering students to find their unique sound while developing strong vocal technique.
Sarabeth holds degrees from UCLA and USC and is committed to both her craft and her students, combining performance excellence with passionate mentorship to inspire the next generation of singers.

content.value: Voice Lessons

I am a proud alumnus of both UCLA and USC, holding a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in Vocal Performance. Currently, I serve on the faculty at USC’s School of Dramatic Arts, where I teach private voice lessons.

As a voice teacher based in Los Angeles, I draw on both my academic training and my professional experience as an opera singer to help students build healthy, sustainable vocal technique. My teaching focuses on developing ease and freedom in the voice while refining the instrument for classical and musical theater repertoire.

Students will strengthen their head and mix voices by learning to apply proper breath pressure and vocal resonance. We also work on chest voice function to support a balanced mix and a healthy, resonant belt that avoids over-compression.

In addition to technique, I emphasize diction, artistry, performance practice, and audition preparation. Each lesson is typically split between technical exercises and focused work on repertoire selected by the student. I believe in using classical technique as a strong foundation that supports vocal clarity and stamina across all musical styles.
All sessions are private and one-on-one, best suited for teens and adults who are serious about developing their voice.

I am a member of the National Association of Teachers of Singing and have completed advanced training through the Westminster Choir College Vocal Pedagogy Institute and the New York Voice Teacher Training program.
Teaching Philosophy
Hello, fellow singers!
I offer private voice lessons in Los Angeles with the goal of helping you discover and refine your unique instrument, and to express powerful stories and artistry through your voice.

My musical journey began with piano lessons and singing in my church and school choirs. From a young age, I was drawn to classical music, and it wasn’t long before I fell completely in love with opera. I began classical voice lessons at age 15 and quickly realized that opera was where my heart truly belonged, inspiring me to pursue it more deeply.

I earned my Bachelor of Arts in Opera Performance from UCLA and went on to receive my Master’s degree in Vocal Performance from USC. I currently serve on faculty at USC’s School of Dramatic Arts, where I teach private voice lessons to musical theater students.
Since graduating, I’ve continued to perform in professional opera productions, and I’ve also grown a deep passion for teaching and continue to hone my craft as a voice teacher.

I believe singing should feel natural, easy, and free. My teaching philosophy centers on helping each student find their authentic voice through science-informed, sustainable technique—focusing on clear vowels, consistent breath flow, and full-body support. I use classical technique as a strong foundation that supports singing in all musical styles, including musical theater, and I take a scientific approach to understanding how the voice functions to ensure efficient, healthy vocal production.
Lessons are held either at my home studio in Altadena or online via Zoom, depending on your preference and location.
Ready to take your singing to the next level? Contact me today to schedule your first lesson!
Attendance
Student is expected to show up on time. If the student finds themselves running late, they will notify Sarabeth.
Students will provide
digital
copies of all music they plan to work on.
Students will be prepared for each lesson with goals for that session.
General
All canceled lessons within 24 hour period of the lesson will be charged.
All paid and charged lessons are nonrefundable.
If you would like to reschedule a lesson, arrangements must be made with Sarabeth Belon prior to the 24-hour period of the scheduled lesson.
There are no makeup lessons.
Frequent cancellations will result in the student being dropped from their repeating time slot.
This policy will be strictly enforced to respect the time and limited availability of Sarabeth Belon.
Payment methods
: Credit Card
I post tips & tricks @sarabethstudio and check out what I have been up to @sarabethbelon!

Piano & Vocal Coach

Piano Teacher

Piano & Vocal Coach

June 2016 - Present - Sarabeth's Studio
Owner & Voice Teacher

Nov 2016 - Jun 2018 – US Music Lessons
Vocal Coach & Piano Teacher

University of Southern California - Master of Music in Vocal Arts Performance 2021-2023
Westminister College of the Arts - Vocal Pedagogy Institute 2021
